qa_pairs.question: How do users get access to the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must opt-in on the CCDB website to use the Trillium cluster. The specific webpage for this is https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a user opted into Niagara or Mist before August 5, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Users who had opted into Niagara or Mist on August 5, 2025, will have automatically been opted-in to Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: What does opting into Trillium provide access to?
qa_pairs.answer: Opting into Trillium gives you access to both the CPU and GPU subclusters of Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: How can I get access to HPSS for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: To get access to HPSS, the nearline system for Trillium, there is a separate opt-in process on https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems.

qa_pairs.question: Will I automatically get HPSS access for Trillium if I had it for Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you had access to HPSS through Niagara before August 5th, you will be opted-in automatically for HPSS access with Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: Has HPSS been integrated into Trillium yet?
qa_pairs.answer: No, for the moment, HPSS has not been integrated into Trillium yet.

qa_pairs.question: Will files on HPSS change once it's integrated with Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, once HPSS has been integrated into Trillium, the files on HPSS will not have changed as it is still the same storage system.

qa_pairs.question: How do I log in to the CPU subcluster of Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: You can log in to the CPU subcluster of Trillium by SSH-ing to `trillium.alliancecan.ca` using the command `$ ssh USERNAME@trillium.alliancecan.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What security requirements are necessary for logging into Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: You will need to use SSH Keys and have Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled on your CCDB account to log in to Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do the first time I log into Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The first time you log in, please make sure you are actually accessing Trillium by checking if the login node SSH host key fingerprint matches.

qa_pairs.question: What are the CPU login nodes on Trillium called?
qa_pairs.answer: The CPU login nodes are called tri-login01-6.

qa_pairs.question: What are the limitations of the Trillium CPU login nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: The CPU login nodes do not have GPUs and can only submit jobs to CPU compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: How do I log in to the GPU subcluster of Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: To access the GPU subcluster of Trillium, you should log into `trillium-gpu.alliancecan.ca` using the command `$ ssh USERNAME@trillium-gpu.alliancecan.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the GPU login node on Trillium called?
qa_pairs.answer: The GPU login node is called trig-login01.

qa_pairs.question: What hardware does the Trillium GPU login node possess?
qa_pairs.answer: The Trillium GPU login node, trig-login01, has 4 NVIDIA H100 GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What are the limitations of the Trillium GPU login node?
qa_pairs.answer: From the GPU login node, you can only submit to the compute nodes of the GPU subcluster.

qa_pairs.question: What will I see in my home directory after logging into Trillium for the first time?
qa_pairs.answer: After logging in for the first time, you will see the files that were on Niagara.

qa_pairs.question: What command is highly recommended to run after first logging into Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: It is highly recommended that you run the command `$ trisetup` after logging in for the first time.

qa_pairs.question: What does the `trisetup` command do?
qa_pairs.answer: The `trisetup` command populates your home directory with files like .bashrc, .bash_profile, .chsrc, and .Xauthority, and creates directories such as .licenses, .local, .ssh, and links (containing symbolic links to scratch and project directories). It also saves original versions of these files with a date stamp.

qa_pairs.question: What actions should be taken after running `trisetup`?
qa_pairs.answer: After running `trisetup`, you can recompile your code and reinstall virtual environments.

qa_pairs.question: What is the current status of the SciNet OnDemand website for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The SciNet OnDemand website (https://ondemand.scinet.utoronto.ca) will remain connected to Niagara for now.

qa_pairs.question: What will the SciNet OnDemand website eventually offer for Trillium users?
qa_pairs.answer: The SciNet OnDemand website will eventually offer a way to log into Trillium and run web-based apps like Jupyter with access to the Trillium file system.

qa_pairs.question: How many homogeneous subclusters does Trillium consist of?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium consists of two homogeneous subclusters: a CPU subcluster and a GPU subcluster.

qa_pairs.question: How many cores does each compute node in the Trillium CPU subcluster have?
qa_pairs.answer: Each compute node of the Trillium CPU subcluster has 192 cores.

qa_pairs.question: How much available memory does each compute node in the Trillium CPU subcluster have?
qa_pairs.answer: Each compute node of the Trillium CPU subcluster has 755 GB of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What type of CPUs are used in the Trillium CPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The CPUs in the Trillium CPU subcluster are AMD Zen 5 chips, also known as Turin.

qa_pairs.question: How many compute nodes are in the Trillium CPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 1224 compute nodes in the Trillium CPU subcluster.

qa_pairs.question: What is the total number of cores in the Trillium CPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The Trillium CPU subcluster has a total of 235,008 cores.

qa_pairs.question: What is suggested for code that used Intel MKL on Niagara for math and linear algebra routines?
qa_pairs.answer: It is suggested to switch to Flexiblas or use the AMD AOCL libraries directly for code that used math and linear algebra routines from the Intel MKL.

qa_pairs.question: Where can the AMD AOCL libraries be found?
qa_pairs.answer: The AMD AOCL libraries are available in the `aocl-blas` and `aocl-lapack` modules.

qa_pairs.question: How many cores does each compute node in the Trillium GPU subcluster have?
qa_pairs.answer: Each compute node in the GPU subcluster has 96 cores.

qa_pairs.question: How much available memory does each compute node in the Trillium GPU subcluster have?
qa_pairs.answer: Each compute node in the GPU subcluster has 755 GB of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: How many GPUs does each compute node in the Trillium GPU subcluster have?
qa_pairs.answer: Each compute node in the GPU subcluster has 4 GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What type of CPUs are used in the Trillium GPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The CPUs in the Trillium GPU subcluster are AMD Zen 4 chips, also known as Genoa.

qa_pairs.question: What type of GPUs are used in the Trillium GPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The GPUs in the Trillium GPU subcluster are NVIDIA H100.

qa_pairs.question: How many GPU compute nodes are there in Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 61 GPU compute nodes in Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: What is the total number of GPUs in Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: In total, Trillium has 244 GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What is important to ensure regarding resource usage for jobs on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: It is important to make sure that your jobs are not wasting resources; they should either use all of the cores, exhaust most of the memory, or efficiently use GPUs.
